mission: Determine whether the two residual Paper 35 portal debts collapse further or are irreducibly independent on the current stack.

theorem_1_coefficient_data_nonuniqueness.statement: Even after target reduction inside the type-I portal class, the Yukawa/Majorana data remain positive-dimensional. Therefore coefficient selection is a genuine remaining theorem debt and cannot be eliminated by the existing target constraints.

theorem_2_source_abundance_nonuniqueness.imported_missing_object: V_tr: a charged transfer interaction generator from the neutral collective carrier to a visible charged/B-L sector
theorem_2_source_abundance_nonuniqueness.statement: At fixed portal coefficient data (Y,M), the current stack still permits multiple admissible nonthermal source-abundance histories because no support-branch production theorem exists. Distinct histories produce distinct visible yields through the same portal class. Therefore source abundance is a genuine remaining theorem debt.

theorem_3_twin_debt_irreducibility.statement: The two remaining Paper 35 portal debts are irreducible on the current stack. Coefficient data (Y,M) live in the visible transfer interaction sector, while source abundance f_N lives in the neutral support-branch production sector. No current theorem maps one to the other, and explicit nonuniqueness survives separately on both sides.
exact_remaining_objects: a theorem selecting Yukawa/Majorana coefficient data Y,M from IO boundary data
a theorem deriving the nonthermal support-branch source-abundance history f_N

final_boundary.safe_statement: No further theorem-grade reduction remains on the current stack after portal-class closure. The Paper 35 baryogenesis line is exhausted at two irreducible residual theorems: coefficient selection and nonthermal source abundance.
